Title: GMCP Contract Extension
Description: The proposed implementation plan ensures completion of the new radio network prior to the expiry of GMCP agreement. IF the implementation schedule is delayed, THEN support for the legacy system will be required beyond the current GMCP agreement.

Title: Civil Infrastructure Costs
Description: As the LMRN bid did not include the costs of bettering ministry assets the LMRN supplier will be paid for this work separately. IF the costs for this work exceed current rough-order-of-magnitude (ROM) estimates, THEN the LMRN budget may be exceeded.
Mitigation Plan: This is a generic TB submission risk that will persist throughout the duration of the project and will be addressed on an as needed basis should the risk evolve into an issue. Accordingly, the 'due date' for this risk has been pushed out to end of fiscal 2023-24 for the time being and may be revisited as necessary.

Title: LMRN Network Design Changes
Description: IF the project team makes significant design changes during the detailed design review, THEN the fiscal plan may be impacted.
Mitigation Plan: This is a generic TB submission risk that will persist throughout the duration of the project and will be addressed on an as needed basis should the risk evolve into an issue. Accordingly, the 'due date' for this risk has been pushed out to end of fiscal 2023-24 for the time being and may be revisited as necessary.

Title: Capital and Operating Fiscal Profile
Description: The ministry has presented the capital and operating budget based on the transition plan proposed by Bell Mobility. IF the implementation plan changes during the design phase, THEN the forecasted timing of the capital spend and annual payment will be impacted.
Mitigation Plan: If significant changes are required in year, SOLGEN may do an In Year Report Back outside of the MYP cycle.

Title: Currency Fluctuations and Tariffs
Description: IF there are any significant changes between the Canadian and US dollar exchange rate or if trade restrictions are subsequently implemented, THEN the cost of LMRN could be negatively impacted.
Mitigation Plan: This is a generic TB submission risk that will persist throughout the duration of the project and will be addressed on an as needed basis should the risk evolve into an issue. Accordingly, the 'due date' for this risk has been pushed out to end of fiscal 2023-24 for the time being and may be revisited as necessary.

Title: Delayed Payment Approvals for Civil Infrastructure Invoices
Description: The LMRN build and transition is supported by an aggressive civil infrastructure build/reinforcement schedule for ministry owned tower sites managed by multiple Construction Statement of Works (CSOWs).   IF approvals for CSOWs are delayed, THEN there will be a negative impact to the transition schedule as well as non-compliance with the Ontario legislation ÃÂConstruction ActÃÂ which mandates timely payment for construction work.
Mitigation Plan: This is a generic TB submission risk that will persist throughout the duration of the project and will be addressed on an as needed basis should the risk evolve into an issue. Accordingly, the 'due date' for this risk has been pushed out to end of fiscal 2023-24 for the time being and may be revisited as necessary.

Title: Civil Site Infrastructure Readiness
Description: IF ministry tower infrastructure is not able to support the new equipment, THEN the time to replace these towers may negatively impact project schedule and budget.

Title: Client Ministry Site and User Transition Readiness
Description: Migration to the new network requires all emergency services within a zone to transition at the same time.  Meaning all client ministries (OPP, MOH, MTO, MNRF etc.) and their subsequent user groups and systems must be fully deployed, trained and prepared to make the transition.   IF a given ministry is not ready at the planned transition point, THEN the entire transition plan may be impacted.
Mitigation Plan: The revised migration plan implies a staggered approach to client ministry transition so it is anticipated that the risk as originally articulated will be mitigated.  However, this risk will be left open for monitoring purposes with a plan to revist the risk by the end of the 2024 calendar year (after SWZ transition).

Title: New Client Ministry Requirements
Description: Public safety requirements are never stagnant and as such client ministry requirements continue to grow. This evolution will continue throughout the LMRN implementation.  New ambulance base openings, new OPP local policing agreements and changes to highway maintenance contracts are just a few examples of on-going changes.   IF new requirements cannot be easily accommodated, THEN both the project schedule and budget may be adversely impacted.
Mitigation Plan: This is a generic TB submission risk that will persist throughout the duration of the project and will be addressed on an as needed basis should the risk evolve into an issue. Accordingly, the 'due date' for this risk has been pushed out to end of fiscal 2023-24 for the time being and may be revisited as necessary.

Title: New Feature Enablement
Description: The LMRN network provides client ministries with new features and benefits. IF enabling these new features requires additional resources and updates to applications, THEN client ministries may incur unforeseen costs, may have complicated transition schedules, and may not be able to perform test and training requirements.
Mitigation Plan: This is a generic TB submission risk that will persist throughout the duration of the project and will be addressed on an as needed basis should the risk evolve into an issue. Accordingly, the 'due date' for this risk has been pushed out to end of fiscal 2023-24 for the time being and may be revisited as necessary.

Title: ISEDC Licensing
Description: Certain solutions proposed by the supplier rely on the ministry obtaining new microwave frequency licensing from the federal regulator. IF the regulator does not grant licences for these solutions, THEN the alternative for achieving these requirements will likely result in a cost increase. Note: for mobile bands, there are no province-wide frequencies available.

Title: Impact to capital and operating planning due to variability of project schedule
Description: The PSRN integrated project plan relies on multiple vendors and 10 different Ministry program areas, which will increase variability of the timing for delivery of project deliverables. IF this variability in timing of vendor deliverables varies significantly from original timelines, THEN both capital and operating planning may be impacted as deliverables are delayed outside of monthly, quarterly, and fiscal forecasts.
Mitigation Plan: This is a generic TB submission risk that will persist throughout the duration of the project and will be addressed on an as needed basis should the risk evolve into an issue. Accordingly, the 'due date' for this risk has been pushed out to end of fiscal 2023-24 for the time being and may be revisited as necessary.

Title: LMRN System Capacity
Description: Current system limitations on the LMRN system (Motorola Astro P25 product) has created a risk of inability to scale up the system in future. (1) Site Capacity for Zone (North) is currently at 96% of total Sites aloowed (2) Channel Capacity of Zone 2 (North) is corrent at 87% of toatl available capoacity and with AFFES non-voted design will be 96% of the total available capacity. The above created a risk of very limits expansion/ scale up capbily for the system in furture.   Discussions are ongoing with Bell (suported by Motorola) and a final capacity analysis is pending.
Mitigation Plan: 2022/03/22 - This risk will need to be monitored / tracked till a premanent solution in terms of an additonal core is implemented (currently being planned). 2021/10/28 - AFFES has provided formal notice that Receive Voting technology will not work for their method of operations for the sustained action channels and regional channel layers. The RISK is being coverted to an ISSUE. Alternate soluton option will beed to be explored for system capacity limitation considerations.  2021/09/23 - TR requested for ROM pricing for additonal Zone Core to be able to define options. 2021/09/08 - TR is developing a presentation with solution options for decisioin makers.  2021/08/26 - Bell continues to conduct analysis based on GMCB inputs. A collaboration session sceduled for Sep 3 for next steps.  20-Aug-21-DK-TO be discussed with Kellie prior to moving forward. Corinna to add to the agenda for Tuesday. 2021/08/18 - Ongoing discussion with Bell to define solutioin options.  Option selected may have high impacts to scope, schedule and cost.  _____________ Some suggested strategies include: (1) Mitigation strategy for total site count limit :  - Move 41 Parks from North to South Zone that will impact WAN design  - Use one or more C-Subs for these Parks which could reduce total used capacity to 83%  (2) Mitigation strategy for  channel count limitation:  - Add another Zone to LMRN system - Remove BBAS from Communication Centre CCGWs - Reduces total channel count by 46 in the North Zone reducing total capacity used to 83% (SA voted) and 92% (SA non voted)
Raised By: Arnab Nandy on behalf of TR

Accordingly, its decided that GMCB will contine to use the PDR8000 product for the PCCs. As an alternative, Bell is looking in the possibility of replacing the PDR8000 with GTR8000 for the PCCs at no additonal cost to GMCB. GMCB apporves the alternate apporach as well.

IF the variance application is denied THEN the backup generators will be red-tagged and not allowed to operate which means that backup power will not be available for the affected sites.
Mitigation Plan: Here is the mitigation plan: 1.    Check the possibility of extension of the variance application 2.    Expedite the installation of generators in the South Zone by November 2021. 3.    The CIR stream will submit CN/CO against GMCP to attach old shelters to new shelters ÃÂ TR has confirmed new large RIE generators will provide backup power to run both.  23-Jun-22: Bell has completed inspection ofÃÂ SWZ and NWZ sites. NEZ sitesÃÂ to be scheduled. 29-Apr-22: GMCB is waiting for Bell to complete the fuel delivery inspection.  18-Mar-22: TSSA inspector conducted the five site visits and have noted a few anomalies.  Bell generator management group is looking into these issues. 3-Mar-22:  Formal approval received from TSSA. We are conducting 5 inspections visits next week in line with the variance approvals.The signed letter is posted at (F:\GMCP\Operations\TSSA Compliances\Approval - GMCB 118 sites (2073238 et al) - 2022-02-17 - Signed). Based on discussion with Andrew, the risk will be closed after the site visits are done and a clean approval is received,  2-Feb-22: TSSA  committee has approved GMCB variance applications for all the sites (118) with the amended transition dates as : SWZ ÃÂ 2026-03-31 SEZ ÃÂ 2028-03-31 NEZ ÃÂ 2029-03-31 NWZ ÃÂ 2029-03-31 This should provide us with ample timing for transition.  A formal notification is expected in the next few weeks. The risk will be closed on receipt of the letter.  28-Jan-22:  GMCB has asked for an extension till 2027/28 and waiting for a response from TSSA 14-Dec-21: No update- The new engineer is playing catch-up on the active files. 29-Nov-21: Jeff Cook continues to follow up with TSSA. A formal confirmation regarding the variance application is still awaited 15-Oct-21: No updates 6-Oct-21: No updates 29-Sep-21: No updates 21-Sep-21: Formal response awaited. 14-Sep-21: No updates 9-Sep-21: Formal response awaited from TSSA  1-Sep-21:  TSSA official has advised via email that  inspectors' assignment is a work in progress. A formal response to the extension of variance application is awaited.  27-Aug-21: TSSA has advised that there should not be any issues in extending the variance application deadline as long as GMCB  continues with the processes of inspections, alarming and remedial repairs as required until the new generators have been installed and inspected by TSSA.  An official response is expected in the next few weeks after TSSA completes the paperwork for the extension of the variance application.  24-Aug-21: Follow-up continues. Mitigation Plan: 27/Aug. CP77- eSMT Email exchange function will be used to notify Bell about Charnge Request and receiving response to CRQ from Bell. The responses from Bell will be logged directly into the Change ticket log. The functionality has been tested in advance of MTO-PHM roll out. Title: STRM-R0001: Equipment at Civil Sites prior to Transition
Description: A large volume of equipment will be at Civil sites prior to transition.ÃÂ  There is a gap in understanding how these assets will be managed / monitored.
Mitigation Plan:
2020-09-18: After DG3 completion, the equipment at the FP sites is owned by Bell. The assumption is that asset ownership is tied to substantial completion and the associated payment to Bell (i.e. sites following FP). Christina suggests that we have basic heart beat measures / monitoring in place (where possible - backhaul needs to be in place). Team approach - Bell owns the liability for the network gear under LMRN (not RIE which we own) - GMCB will not ask for monitoring on network gear specifically (this would trigger compensation).

2020-09-11: PCF speaking with legal at 3:00 pm today to confirm ownership (specific to DG3). Revisit next week. Broader conversation required to cover zone transition, substantial completion and monitoring commitments. Alignment at DSC required (Risk & Issues meeting first - standard process).

2020-08-28: There needs to be a clear understanding on who will be responsible for the assets that will be deployed at Civil sites and that will, in many cases, be at the sites for some time prior to Transition.  Evelyn communicated that Steve M, feels that the assets are the responsibility of BMR until a given Zone is completed (Transition?).  Arnab will look at this from a contract perspective and will also ensure that there is clarity around the language used.

Title: MOH audio tuning support for ABCA installations
Description:
As part of the ABCA installation, the RSTP stipulates that the BAS solution is tested to confirm that the radio can transit over the network and audio is received. 

Unlike other Ministries, MOH has a unique paging solution that transmits escalating tones over the network that are played over the PA system. In order to demonstrate the escalating tones at the base and adjust the audio settings of the PA system to support transition readiness, MOH would like to test the end to end solution. 

Testing the end to end solution will require access to an LMRN console that has a config file with the corresponding base talk group, MOH agency encryption, and require a resource to initiate the page to support the testing. 
Decision needs to be made in terms of:
		- Will BMR provide LMRN programmed console 
		- Where will the LMRN console be located (GDC?)
		- Who will execute the tests (MOH?)
		- Who will support the testing in case of issues?

As this investigation may not be concluded ahead of the H&S deadline, GMCB is assessing all available mitigation options including a decommissioning or temporary loweringÃÂ of the tower in order to mitigate the H&S risk. Title: AFFFES schedule may be impacted by delay in other PSRN zones
Description: "If the deployment schedule for other zones are delayed,  Then the AFFES schedule will be impacted and may exceed the communicated end date of the PSRN project"
Mitigation Plan: Discussion underway to align on PSRN schedule for all zones, targeting completion of AFFES by March 30 2027.

Title: Potental AFFFES redesign work may be required if the vendor does not align with the AFFES site selection
Description: If the vendor identifies any issues (technical/contractual) with the selected AFFES sites or channels, then redesign work and site selection changes may be required
Mitigation Plan: Ongoing discussions at the engineering level to ensure vendor is aware of AFFES site selection which is underway with the client. RFC to be prepared to formally communicate final AFFES site selection.

Title: If the regulator (ISED Canada) does not approve the licenses
Description: If the regulator (ISED Canada) does not approve the licenses required to support the AFFES channel plan, then redesign work and site selection changes may be required
Mitigation Plan: Approval of 11 air-to-ground channels received. GMCB will engage the regulator for formal approval of the complete channel plan once it has been finalized. Risk to be monitored until channel plan is fully approved.

Title: If the number of additional VHF frequencies which can be made available to AFFES is limited
Description: Limited availability of additional VHF (Very High Frequency) frequencies may restrict the ability to design and implement a radio solution which fully meets AFFES operational requirements, impacting emergency management communications, frontline personnel and public safety.
Mitigation Plan:
GMCB has worked with the regulator to obtain potential air-to-ground channels which may help address channel constraints (if not required for Trunked network)
 - AFFES transition is scheduled after the Trunked network, allowing for an opportunity to assess any additional available frequencies  - Preliminary AFFES channel plan drafted and targeted for review with LRC in April 2022, initial site selection confirmed with finalization underway (dependent on LMRN trunked network plan in SWZ and SEZ)

Title: Availability of channels for AFFES usage may be impacted by trunked network needs
Description: If the operational needs of the trunked network exceed planned channel usage, then availability of channels for AFFES usage may be impacted
Mitigation Plan: GMCB developing AFFES channel in parallel with overall channel plan for trunked network, ensuring any updates to the trunked plan are reflected in the AFFES channel plan and impact addressed. Work underway to find additional channel capacity to support AFFES needs, including review of channel reuse options and potential to license air-to-ground channels at lower altitude (1000 feet).

Title: Site Design Work Completion
Description: If BMR is unable to engage multiple Engineering and Site Survey firms. Engineering and design work may not be completed on time for scaling up for meeting the planned run rates.
Mitigation Plan: BMR is engaging Engineering Design firms as per the Project delivery and Advanced Deployment requirements. GMCB is monitoring the site design work progress to avoid any potential delays.

Title: Site Remediation Work Completion
Description: If remediation work for the OPP detachments with longer lead time is not initiated early and completed on time, the delivery schedule may be impacted for the In-Building Coverage readiness at the OPP detachments.
Mitigation Plan: BMR requested to expedite the site design work and highlight site remediation details as part of the site design package for OPP and Facilities team to initiate the work for timele completion.

Title: Independent Air Ground Design Strategy
Description: If Independent Air Ground Design and Strategy is not finalized and updated in the Fleetmapping for OPP, operational readiness cannot be completed for Advanced Deployment.
Mitigation Plan:
Independent Air Ground Design to be prepared by GMCB as per OPP request. GMCB has initiated conversation with ISED to determine most viable channel options (SIM 1, 700 MHz). GMCB and OPP discussed the SIM 1 channel placement in the Fleetmapping. GMCB has prepared a plan for implementation of Independent Air Ground design and currently under review by GMCB to proceed further. Air Ground design with SIM1 coverage and 64 preliminary sites selection required AFFES site selection is completed on June 30, 2021. SIM1 strategy to be further discussed before final SIM1 preliminary site selection can be shared with OPP, trying not to have SIM1 and AFFES channels at the same sites. Discussion held with ISED and GMCB has secured agreement that ISED will support SIM1 layer. GMCB has applied Aircraft License as first step. GMCB will submit application for Base Station License as second step. First step may require min 2 months for international co-ordination. OPP to work on how sites would be voted (groups) and how SUs could be programmed ÃÂ¢ÃÂÃÂ each group programmed as a unique channel, or all groups programmed as the same channel. OPP will ensure Fleetmapping document updates for SUs as per the recommended approach for the Aircraft Radios. SIM1 VHF channel is not available and being used by Quebec Govt. GMCB is planning to use 700 MHz channel as a solution for the Independent Air Ground Design. Meeting held at GDC on Aug 12, 2021 for discussion and solutioning. Meeting held at GDC during week of Aug 30, 2021 for solutioning and finalization of the design. GMCB is escalating for exploring the option of sharing the SIM1 VHF channel. This may require an update to the LMRN Conventional Channel Plan if the sharing option is not feasible. GMCB and OPP agreed to mitigate the risk by updating Fleetmapping and Conventional Channel Plan in the most optimal manner to capture the future outcomes of the sharing option request. GMCB has received a contact in Quebec Government and initiated discussion for exploring the option of sharing the SIM1 VHF channel with the Quebec users. Quebec Government is concerned over the potential interference to their Conservation Officers. OPP will review and plan conducting testing using Aeronautical Radio and Antennas with support from Aviation team. OPP to discuss internally and evaluate if 700 MHz design is an alternate to VHF. GMCB to review the BMR Change Request and modify for the 700 MHz Design if the decision is made to proceed with 700 MHz Infrastructure.
OPP has decided to use 7AIR03 and remain optimistic about future improvements with SIM11. Completed on Nov 09, 2021.

Title: Funding for FDVR Spares
Description:
If spare FDVR equipment for casinos are not procured, then service for the OPP and Rama PS will be affected in the event of FDVR failure.
Also applies to Pt Edward Casino.
Mitigation Plan: Secure funding in next fiscal year and proceed to procure spare FDVR for each casino.

Title: Training Equipment Delivery
Description: If training equipment delivery times are not confirmed by BMR soon enough and scheduled far enough in advance of Advanced Deployment, then end user training planning and execution cannot be completed and the AD schedule is at risk
Mitigation Plan: Seek an ETA from BMR as soon as possible and adjust AD planning accordingly

Title: On site console training capability
Description: if a suitable solution to allow the OPP to provide end user console training at the Comm Center locations is not found, end user training will be very costly and take far more time, putting the transition plan at risk
Mitigation Plan: GMCB working with Bell on adjustments to the contract to allow consoles to be set up in a meeting room in advance of transition with near-full functionality to allow for onsite training.

Title: Funding for PDR8000 Spares
Description: If spare PDR8000 equipment for PCCs are not procured, then fallback option (when PCC loses connectivity) will be affected in the event of PDR8000 failure because PCC system resource on LMRN console may not be avaialble.
Mitigation Plan: Secure funding in next fiscal year and proceed to procure spare PDR8000 for each PCC.

Title: OPP BEAT DVRS Update
Description: If a Mobile DVR is parked close to a OPP BEAT site, on the same talk group, then the potential exists for a problem on LMRN (NOTE - no impact on Advanced Deployment).
Mitigation Plan: As a standard maintenance process, Bell would upgrade the firmware on these systems to ensure the latest fixes are applied.

Title: SDD Document Revision due to concrete or asphalt on site
Description: SDDs submitted by BMR with concrete or asphalt on site may require a change subsequent to the site visit by IO for the remediation work completion.
Mitigation Plan: All SDDs are submitted with the required information including concrete and asphalt for further assessment by IO. Close monitoring of the SDDs will be carried out by GMCB to avoid delays in cutting the asphalt or concrete.

Title: UE deployment plan delay due to contract amendment
Description: If the contract amendment is not completed on time, the RFCs included in the contract amendment may potentially impact the equipment ordering and delivery by BMR. Ministry Partners have requested for changes to the UE packages that will require BMR to initiate ordering of the components to build and deliver.
Mitigation Plan: Monitor closely the contract amendment completion timeline.

Client ministries walked through the Transition Approach Document. Feedback from client ministry stakeholders was gathered and incorporated into the document.   Contractually the document has to be agreed by all client ministries by 16-Dec (ID:79354). [new] The contractual date couldn't be met [old] Based on slow responses from some stakeholders meeting the date of 16th Dec is at risk.
Mitigation Plan: Client Ministries to be requested for expedited responses.

Decision Required: Who will be responsible to take care of decommissioning after transition? Decommissioning locations:-        - Network Sites (GMCP Trunking)        - Client Ministry Sites - MSRD deployed site locations - (AMB, Comm Centres, CACCS, PCCs, TIS...)

Title: CO/CNs impact on Transition
Description: What are the CO/CNs that are expected to complete just before the planned transition dates or expected to compete during transition? Impact of ongoing/planned CO/CNs on transition? What is the suggested course of action to handle all such CO/CNs from Transition perspective?   Specially - Seasonal AMB

Title: Remote Access to CADi
Description: OPP wants to have remote access to CADi. OPP to be convinced that this remote access was not an identified RFB requirement and while GMCB is striving to implement it, OPP ÃÂshouldÃÂ not make this a transition requirement.
Mitigation Plan: OPP to be convinced that this remote access was not an identified RFB requirement and while GMCB is striving to implement it, OPP ÃÂshouldÃÂ not make this a transition requirement.

Title: Sites not functioning as intended after Transition
Description: Coverage and capacity can get impacted due to any malfunctioning or incident immediately after transition
Mitigation Plan: Mitigation-1: Transportable sites (CoWs) can be made available in each zone before commencement of transition. Should be made part of the transition plan accordingly. Mitigation-2: Resource availability for on site troubleshooting

Title: Zone roaming during transition timeframe
Description: User from non-transitioned area with non-upgraded equipment roaming in transitioned area will not have any coverage
Mitigation Plan: Mitigation-1: The SIM11 and PCOM can be used by OPP and MoH. SIM11 and PCOM will still be analog and transitioned to LMRN P-25 once all zones are transitioned.  Mitigation-2: Advance deploy SUs in areas adjacent to transitioned areas that are likely to travel into transitioned zone.   Mitigation-3: All other ministries; allocate some common pole of spare LMRN radios and can be temporarily allocated to resource/s planning to visit the transitioned zone.

Title: Transition Implementation Plan Development
Description: BMR and Client Ministry resource availability for transition implementation plan development.
Mitigation Plan: Mitigation-1: BMR and Client Ministry teams to be pushed at appropriate levels to ramp-up their transition support team and resources

Title: Transition Implementation Plan Development
Description: Transition Implementation plan creation was planned for completion by 12-Jun for Group-2 (OPP) and by 21-Jun for Group-4 (MoH) Client Ministries. However, due to required pending information & feedback from OPP, and floor plans from MoH achieving planned dates is at high risk.   Transition implementation plans for all the Groups must be completed well before planned transition Gate-1 session.   Related contract milestones unique numbers: 28165, 28166, 28167, 28168.
Mitigation Plan: Matter escalated directly to client ministries as well as via PLs to get an expedited response. GMCB management request to assistance as well.

Description: Site access delay due to seasonal activities
